questions.question: Where was the 34th session of the International Maritime Organisation (IMO) held?
questions.explanation: India has been re-elected to the Council of the International Maritime Organisation (IMO) for 2026–27. The 34th session of the IMO Assembly was held in London, where the elections took place. India received the highest vote tally in the category of ten countries with the largest interest in international seaborne trade. It secured 154 out of 169 valid votes cast during the election.

questions.question: Who was named the World Athlete of the Year (Men) in 2025?
questions.explanation: Armand Duplantis (Sweden) and Sydney McLaughlin-Levrone (USA) were named World Athletes of the Year 2025 at the World Athletics Awards. Duplantis broke the men’s pole vault world record four times and won all 16 competitions, including indoor and outdoor world titles. He is the first male pole vaulter in modern athletics to remain undefeated in a single event for two consecutive years. McLaughlin-Levrone won the women’s 400 metre title in Tokyo in 47.78 seconds, breaking a 42-year-old World Championships record. She is the first athlete to win world titles in both 400 metre flat and 400 metre hurdles.

questions.question: Which word was selected as the Word of the Year 2025 by Oxford University Press?
questions.explanation: Rage bait was named the Word of the Year 2025 by Oxford University Press (OUP). Its online usage rose three times, showing changes in how people seek attention and engagement. It means online content made to provoke anger or outrage to increase views and traffic. The term first appeared in 2002 on Usenet with a different meaning linked to road rage. It later became popular in social media and digital journalism. It beat shortlisted words like aura farming and biohack. Rage bait boosts clicks and reactions, especially in politics and viral trends. Its advanced form is called rage farming, where anger is used repeatedly to drive engagement, often using misinformation.

questions.question: What is the name of the scheme implemented by the government through the Tea Board for development and promotion of tea across India?
questions.explanation: The Government of India, through the Tea Board of India, is implementing the Tea Development & Promotion Scheme (TDPS) in Assam to boost tea production, quality, exports, and small grower income. From 2021–22 to 2025–26, ₹152.76 crore was allocated and ₹150.20 crore was used in Assam. The scheme supported replantation in 437.42 hectares, formed 318 Self Help Groups (SHGs), 143 Farmer Producer Organizations (FPOs), and 26 Farmer Producer Companies (FPCs). It helped set up 31 mini tea factories, converted 30.32 hectares to organic tea, and ran 1,343 capacity-building programmes. Indian tea exports rose from USD 751.07 million (2021–22) to USD 923.89 million (2024–25).

questions.question: Which state is the host of Global Energy Leaders’ Summit (GELS) 2025?
questions.explanation: The Government of Odisha hosts the Global Energy Leaders’ Summit (GELS) 2025 in Puri, Odisha, from 5 to 7 December 2025. The theme is “Powering India: Sufficiency, Balance, Innovation.” The summit focuses on adequate power supply, ecological–economic balance, and innovation in the energy sector. The event promotes sustainable growth and energy transition using advanced digital technologies.

questions.question: Ebola disease, that was recently seen in news, is caused by which agent?
questions.explanation: The Ebola outbreak in the Democratic Republic of Congo (DRC) is declared over after 42 days without new cases, confirmed by Democratic Republic of Congo (DRC) health officials and the World Health Organization (WHO). Ebola, caused by orthoebolaviruses, first discovered in 1976 in DRC near the Ebola River, leads to severe hemorrhagic fever with internal bleeding. It spreads from wild animals like fruit bats and non-human primates, and among humans through direct contact with infected body fluids, not through air.

questions.question: World Soil Day is observed annually on which day?
questions.explanation: World Soil Day is observed annually on 5 December to highlight the importance of healthy soil for food security, ecosystems, and human well-being. The theme for World Soil Day 2025 is “Healthy Soils for Healthy Cities”, focusing on the role of urban soils. Urban soils face pressures from rapid urbanisation, soil sealing, pollution, and loss of organic matter. Healthy soils in cities support food production, water purification, carbon storage, temperature regulation, and biodiversity conservation. Preserving soil health is essential for climate resilience, urban planning, and sustainable development.

questions.question: Which state has mandated all thermal power plants to blend 5-7% bamboo biomass or charcoal with coal in December 2025?
questions.explanation: Maharashtra mandated from 2 December 2025 that thermal power plants must blend 5–7% bamboo-based biomass or charcoal with coal under the Maharashtra Bamboo Industry Policy 2025. This makes bamboo a formal part of the state’s energy mix for the first time to cut coal emissions. The government allocated ₹1,534 crore for 2025–2030 and ₹11,797 crore over 20 years to support the transition. Bamboo helps reduce carbon emissions, restore soil, and grow quickly with low inputs. The policy aims to create jobs, boost bamboo industries, and attract green investments and carbon credit markets.

questions.question: Which state government has launched “Sainik Tujhe Salaam” initiative on Armed Forces Flag Day to honour veterans above 90 years?
questions.explanation: Assam Government launched the “Sainik Tujhe Salaam” initiative on Armed Forces Flag Day to honour veterans above 90 years, Veer Naris and Veer Mothers. The programme was attended by Governor Lakshman Prasad Acharya at Lok Bhavan. Tributes were paid to martyrs and serving soldiers across the country. The initiative includes annual home visits by district administration and Zila Sainik Welfare Offices to honour families. Assam supports 41,968 ex-servicemen and 10,361 Veer Naris through veteran welfare offices.

questions.question: Which state government has announced one per cent reservation for orphans in education and government jobs in December 2025?
questions.explanation: Maharashtra Chief Minister Devendra Fadnavis announced one per cent reservation for orphans in education and government jobs. He called the decision a “historic milestone” and a step towards social justice and equal opportunity. The policy was first introduced during his earlier term in 2018. The initiative is guided by Dr. Bhimrao Ramji Ambedkar’s vision of equal access for all sections of society.
